vehicles.trans: 4-speed, 5-speed, 6-speed, 8-speed auto, or hybrid eCVT (varies by generation)
vehicles.slot: Small square cover BEHIND the gear selector (toward rear of console) — NOT at the base. Insert key VERTICALLY.
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot: Small square cover BEHIND the gear selector (toward rear of console) — NOT at the base. Insert key VERTICALLY..
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
vehicles.notes: SLOT IS BEHIND THE SELECTOR — insert key VERTICALLY. Very common mistake.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

knownIssues.title: Shift Lock Slot Is BEHIND the Selector — Not at Base
knownIssues.detail: This is one of the most commonly misidentified shift lock locations in the field. The override slot on the Camry is located BEHIND the gear selector (toward the rear of the car), NOT at the front or side of the selector base. The slot is vertical — insert the key vertically, not at an angle. Many operators probe the wrong area and conclude the override isn't working.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. Hybrid: 12V only.
battery.warnings: SHIFT LOCK SLOT IS BEHIND THE SELECTOR — not at the base. This is the #1 misidentified slot location in the field.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console
shift: This vehicle has a MANUAL TRANSMISSION — no shift lock solenoid exists.
Depress the clutch pedal fully.
Move the gear lever to the Neutral position (center gate, no gear engaged).
Release the clutch. Verify the vehicle rolls freely before towing.
neutral: Manual transmission — no battery power needed to reach Neutral.
Depress the clutch fully.
Move gear lever to Neutral.
Release the clutch and verify the vehicle rolls freely.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. Hybrid: 12V only.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot: Cap near selector base on console.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If using slot without power, apply consistent firm pressure — hybrid selectors may require more force.
Hybrid: 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV battery.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD/Hybrid/PHEV: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. Hybrid/PHEV: 12V only.

vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter (older) or electronic selector slot (4th gen 2024+)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap at side of console shifter (older) or electronic selector slot (4th gen 2024+).
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2WD first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4H — part-time 4WD high range — under 55 MPH on loose surfaces only
4L — low range — stop required to engage, NEVER tow on road in 4L
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH.
Press the 2WD button on the dash.
Confirm the 2WD indicator on the instrument cluster.
Safe to lift rear wheels once confirmed in 2WD.
To exit 4L: stop completely, shift transmission to Neutral, then press 2WD button.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Cap near console shifter (older) or electronic selector slot (newer)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot: Cap near console shifter (older) or electronic selector slot (newer).
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If using slot without power, apply consistent firm pressure — hybrid selectors may require more force.
Hybrid: 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV battery.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2WD first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4WD Lo (4L) — low range — NEVER tow on road in this position
steps: Slow under 3 MPH.
Press the 2WD button on the console.
Confirm 2WD indicator on instrument cluster.
Safe to lift rear wheels once confirmed in 2WD.
3rd gen (2022+) Hybrid: same TC procedure — also 12V only for jumping.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. Hybrid: 12V only.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near selector base on console.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near selector base on console.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Manual floor lever (traditional T-case lever — 4Runner is one of few modern trucks with manual TC lever)
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4H — part-time 4WD high range — under 50 MPH on loose surfaces
4L — low range — stop required, NEVER tow on road in 4L
steps: Stop completely or slow under 3 MPH for 2H/4H shift.
Shift the transmission to Neutral.
Move the TC floor lever firmly to the 2H position.
Confirm the 2H indicator lamp on the dash before lifting rear wheels.
To shift to 4L: stop completely, transmission in Neutral, move lever to 4L.
NEVER tow in 4L on pavement.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

battery.warnings: Manual TC floor lever — stop completely before shifting.

shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot: Cap near selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If using slot without power, apply consistent firm pressure — hybrid selectors may require more force.
Hybrid: 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV battery.

vehicles.slot: JOYSTICK-TYPE selector — cap near joystick base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (Ready mode — do not need full start).
The Prius uses a JOYSTICK-STYLE selector, not a traditional gear lever.
Shift lock slot is JOYSTICK-TYPE selector — cap near joystick base on console.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
While holding slot, press brake and push the joystick to N.
neutral: Apply parking brake.

IMPORTANT — 12V BATTERY IS IN THE TRUNK, not under the hood. TRUNK BATTERY: Open rear hatch, lift right side cargo panel to expose 12V battery. Connect jump pack directly to the battery terminals (red +, black −). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
NEVER touch the HV battery under the rear seat.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift joystick to N normally.
AWD-e models (2019+ 4th gen): FLATBED ONLY — rear electric motor cannot be disabled.
vehicles.notes: 12V battery IN TRUNK. HV battery under rear seat — NEVER touch. Joystick selector.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

knownIssues.title: 12V Battery in Trunk — Not Under Hood
knownIssues.detail: The Prius 12V auxiliary battery is in the TRUNK (cargo area, right side under the panel) — NOT under the hood. Operators who open the hood and find no conventional battery are in the right place but wrong end of the car. Open the rear hatch and lift the cargo floor.

knownIssues.title: Never Access HV Battery Under Rear Seat
knownIssues.detail: The Prius high-voltage hybrid battery is located under the rear seat. It is bright orange-labeled and extremely dangerous. ONLY use the 12V battery in the trunk for any jumping operations.

knownIssues.title: Joystick Gear Selector — No Traditional Lever
knownIssues.detail: The Prius uses a joystick-style selector, not a traditional gear lever. The shift lock release is near the joystick base. Operators expecting a conventional shifter will not recognize it immediately.

battery.location: TRUNK — right side of cargo area under panel. NOT under the hood.
battery.jumpTerminals: In the trunk: lift right cargo panel to expose 12V battery. Red (+) / Black (−) directly on battery.
battery.warnings: Operators who open the hood find no battery. It is IN THE TRUNK. NEVER touch HV battery under rear seat.

The Prius uses a JOYSTICK-STYLE selector, not a traditional gear lever.

While holding slot, press brake and push the joystick to N.
neutral: Apply parking brake.

IMPORTANT — 12V BATTERY IS IN THE TRUNK, not under the hood. TRUNK BATTERY: Open rear hatch, lift right side cargo panel to expose 12V battery. Connect jump pack directly to the battery terminals (red +, black −). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
NEVER touch the HV battery under the rear seat.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift joystick to N normally.
AWD-e models (2019+ 4th gen): FLATBED ONLY — rear electric motor cannot be disabled.

knownIssues.detail: 4th gen Prius with AWD-e has a rear electric motor that cannot be disabled. AWD-e variants are FLATBED ONLY.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (Ready mode — do not need full start).
The Prius uses a JOYSTICK-STYLE selector, not a traditional gear lever.
Shift lock slot is Electronic selector — cap near selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
While holding slot, press brake and push the joystick to N.
neutral: Apply parking brake.

IMPORTANT — 12V BATTERY IS IN THE TRUNK, not under the hood. Open the trunk — battery is trunk — under cargo floor. Connect jump pack. 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.

Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift joystick to N normally.
AWD-e models (2019+ 4th gen): FLATBED ONLY — rear electric motor cannot be disabled.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near selector base on console.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near selector base on console.
vehicles.towMethod: Transfer case to H2 (2WD) first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric push-button on dash — H2 / H4 / L4
positions: H2 — 2WD rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing
L4 — 4WD low range — NEVER tow on road in this position
steps: Stop or slow under 25 MPH.
Press the H2 button on the dash.
Confirm the H2 indicator lamp illuminates.
Safe to lift rear wheels once confirmed in H2.
Shift back to H4 after towing is complete.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base (older) or electronic selector slot (newer)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot: Cap near selector base (older) or electronic selector slot (newer).
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If using slot without power, apply consistent firm pressure — hybrid selectors may require more force.
Hybrid: 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV battery.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2WD first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for road towing
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER tow on road
steps: Slow under 3 MPH.
Press the 2WD button on the console.
Confirm 2WD indicator.
Safe to lift rear wheels.
3rd gen (2023+) Hybrid: same TC procedure — 12V only for jumping.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. 3rd gen Hybrid: 12V only.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot — Electronic selector — slot near selector base.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move the ZF 8HP selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood (shared BMW platform). Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
BMW/ZF 8HP: battery is typically NOT under the hood — under hood (shared bmw platform). Always use the hood jump terminals.
vehicles.notes: Shared platform with BMW Z4. RWD.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed — low clearance, flatbed preferred. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: BMW Z4 shared platform. Use hood terminals. Low clearance — flatbed preferred.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn the Toyota bZ4X to ON/Ready mode.
Shift lock slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move selector to N.
FLATBED ONLY — do not tow with driven wheels on the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood (12V auxiliary). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY.
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.

battery.warnings: NEVER touch HV battery. Flatbed only.
